test_cases.title: Nominal path matches documented behavior
test_cases.steps: Exercise the feature described in this section under normal, in-range conditions.
test_cases.expected_result: Device behaves exactly as described in the section text, with no error condition raised.

test_cases.title: Section text is reflected in user-facing documentation/UI
test_cases.steps: Cross-check the on-device UI strings / user manual wording against this section's current text.
test_cases.expected_result: No contradiction between this section and the on-device behavior or other manual sections that reference it.

test_cases.title: Regression check for this section
test_cases.steps: Re-run prior passing test cases for this section after any firmware change.
test_cases.expected_result: All prior test cases for this section still pass.

test_cases.title: High Blood Pressure Alarm
test_cases.steps: Take a measurement with a systolic value above 180 mmHg
test_cases.expected_result: The device sounds an alarm and displays 'High Blood Pressure' on the screen

test_cases.title: Low Blood Pressure Alarm
test_cases.steps: Take a measurement with a systolic value below 90 mmHg
test_cases.expected_result: The device sounds an alarm and displays 'Low Blood Pressure' on the screen

test_cases.title: Irregular Heartbeat Alarm
test_cases.steps: Simulate an irregular heartbeat during a measurement
test_cases.expected_result: The device sounds an alarm and displays 'Irregular Heartbeat' on the screen

test_cases.title: Error Code for Incorrect Cuff Size
test_cases.steps: Take a measurement with a cuff size that is not within the recommended range (22-42 cm)
test_cases.expected_result: The device displays error code E03 and does not display a blood pressure reading

test_cases.title: Validation of Alarms Presence
test_cases.steps: 1. Power on the device. 2. Check the device's interface for any alarm indicators or menus.
test_cases.expected_result: The device displays an option or section related to alarms.

test_cases.title: Safety Behavior Documentation
test_cases.steps: 1. Refer to the device's manual or documentation. 2. Search for the 'Alarms and Safety Behavior' section.
test_cases.expected_result: The manual contains information or a section titled 'Alarms and Safety Behavior'.

test_cases.title: Alarm and Safety Feature Accessibility
test_cases.steps: 1. Access the device's settings or control panel. 2. Attempt to navigate to the alarms and safety behavior options.
test_cases.expected_result: The alarms and safety behavior options are accessible through the device's interface.

test_cases.title: Overpressure Protection Test
test_cases.steps: 1. Set cuff pressure to 299 mmHg. 2. Verify the device does not trigger the emergency deflation valve. 3. Increase cuff pressure to 300 mmHg for longer than 3 seconds.
test_cases.expected_result: The device triggers the emergency deflation valve, halting inflation and venting the cuff within 2 seconds.

test_cases.title: Error Code E3 Test
test_cases.steps: 1. Simulate an overpressure condition. 2. Verify the device auto-deflates within 2 seconds.
test_cases.expected_result: The device auto-deflates within 2 seconds and displays E3.

test_cases.title: Audible Alarm Test for E3
test_cases.steps: 1. Simulate an overpressure condition (E3). 2. Verify the device sounds an audible alarm.
test_cases.expected_result: The device sounds an audible alarm for E3, which cannot be silenced.

test_cases.title: Error Code E1 Test
test_cases.steps: 1. Simulate a cuff not connected or leak detected condition. 2. Verify the device aborts measurement and displays E1.
test_cases.expected_result: The device aborts measurement and displays E1.

test_cases.title: Error Code E2 Test
test_cases.steps: 1. Simulate a motion artifact detected during measurement condition. 2. Verify the device aborts measurement, displays E2, and prompts retry.
test_cases.expected_result: The device aborts measurement, displays E2, and prompts retry.

source_snapshot.excerpt: If cuff pressure exceeds 299 mmHg at any point, or exceeds 300 mmHg for longer than 3 seconds due to sensor fault, the device immediately triggers an emergency deflation valve, halting inflation and v

The device does not sound an audible alarm for elevated readings by default; audible alarms are limited to the E1-E5 error conditions above ‐and are userconfigurable in the settings menu, except for E
